name: Nibbin
concept: A small, round, ink-blue hedgehog whose spines are chunky well-worn brush nibs, each loaded with a different depth of the same indigo — and who has painted one wobbly signature swirl on its own cream belly by curling up.
imagePrompt:
A chubby painterly hedgehog creature, full body, standing upright on two stubby legs, facing the viewer straight on, symmetrical and centered.

SILHOUETTE: one single connected blob-shaped creature — a fat rounded dome of spines sitting like a hood over a soft pear-shaped little body. The outer edge is clean, definite and readable: a bumpy but BOLD scalloped dome on top (large rounded bumps, never spiky, never thin), curving down into a smooth round belly. Chunky, blunt, confident shapes only. Short thick arms held at its sides, two stubby rounded feet planted flat, one small thick tapered tail attached low. Everything is fused to the body — no floating pieces, no stray hairs, no thin points.

THE ONE IDEA: its spines are not spikes — they are dozens of soft, worn, chubby PAINTBRUSH TIPS. Each spine is a stubby flat brush head: fat, rounded, slightly frayed-flat at the end like a beloved old brush, thick and blunt (nothing needle-fine). They cluster into a dense overlapping dome like an armful of brushes bundled together. Each one is loaded with a different strength of the same ink, so the dome reads as a gradient of tones: pale washed indigo at the front bumps, deepening to near-black ink at the crown. On its cream belly is one wobbly hand-painted spiral swirl in the same ink — clearly painted BY the creature itself, slightly off-center, proud and imperfect, like a signature. Its front right paw is dipped darkest, stained solid ink up to the wrist, the way a child's hand gets when they have been drawing all afternoon.

PALETTE: one bold hero colour — deep indigo ink blue, ranging from stormy midnight at the crown to soft dusty washed periwinkle at the spine tips. Belly, face-front, paws and feet are warm rice-paper cream, faintly toothy and warm. One accent only: a band of muted warm amber-ochre where the spine-dome meets the body, like a soft brass ferrule collar. Muted plum blush on both cheeks. No other colours.

FACE: big, honest, round glossy eyes set wide and low on the face, dark ink-black with one clean soft highlight each — kind, calm, slightly hopeful. A small round dark nose like a drop of ink. A tiny closed upturned smile beneath it. Two small rounded ears peeking from the front of the spine dome. Warm, soft, wants-looking-after.

PROPORTIONS: head and spine dome take up nearly two thirds of the total height. Body low, wide and heavy at the base. Everything soft, weighted, huggable — a creature you could pick up with both hands.

MATERIAL AND RENDERING: painterly. Visible thick gouache and oil brushwork, generous loaded strokes, gentle impasto ridges catching soft light, faint canvas tooth showing through in the cream areas. Edges are painted, not lined — but the outer silhouette stays crisp, definite and unbroken. Soft even studio lighting from the front, gentle ambient occlusion where the spine dome meets the body. Rich, warm, storybook-painting feel.

Nothing finer than a thick brushstroke anywhere. No text, no symbols, no particles, no sparkles.
